reasoning:
Response A is clearly stronger overall because it delivers a mostly coherent, runnable end-to-end design: all required endpoints exist, fan-out uses Channel + BackgroundService, delivery attempts are persisted, HMAC signing is implemented correctly, and retries are configured through Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Resilience on HttpClient. Response B has major functional issues that break the core path (publish -> background delivery), most importantly that DeliveryWorker is registered as a singleton but not as a hosted service, so background processing would not run. B also has correctness risks in delivery-attempt creation logic (possible duplicate attempts against a unique index), retry behavior (retries all non-success including 4xx), and likely compile-time/type mismatch issues around payload types.

A is not perfect: it uses 9.0 package versions despite net10 target, rate limiting is not partitioned per IP, retry count semantics may exceed 5 total tries depending on MaxRetryAttempts interpretation, and graceful shutdown draining is only partial because cancellation can stop channel reading early. Still, compared to B’s critical execution flaws, A better satisfies the required functionality and architecture.

So criterion-by-criterion, A wins most categories decisively.
